name: Overnight Oats
method: Combine the oats, milk and vanilla in a jar or bowl and stir well.
Swirl in the honey, then fold through the yogurt until evenly combined.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours, or overnight.
Top with the blueberries just before serving.

name: Egg White Omelet
method: Whisk the egg whites in a bowl until slightly frothy.
Melt the butter in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Add the mushrooms and bell pepper and saute for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the spinach and cook until just wilted.
Pour in the egg whites, tilting the pan to spread evenly, and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Fold the omelet in half once set and slide onto a plate.

name: Grilled Chicken Salad
method: Season the chicken and grill or pan-sear for 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through.
Rest the chicken for 5 minutes, then slice.
Toss the greens, cherry tomatoes and cucumber together in a large bowl.
Drizzle with olive oil and toss again.
Top the salad with the sliced chicken.

name: Greek Salad with Feta
method: Chop the cucumber, tomato and red onion into bite-sized pieces.
Combine in a bowl with the olives.
Crumble the feta over the top.
Drizzle with olive oil and toss gently to combine.

name: Tuna Pasta
method: Cook the pasta according to packet instructions, then drain and cool slightly.
Finely chop the celery and tomato.
In a large bowl, combine the yogurt, a squeeze of lemon juice, celery and tomato.
Fold in the tuna and the cooked pasta until evenly coated.

name: Quinoa Power Bowl
method: Rinse the quinoa and cook according to packet instructions, then cool slightly.
Whisk the tahini with the juice of the lemon to make a dressing.
Chop the cucumber, tomato and bell pepper.
Combine the quinoa, chickpeas, cucumber, tomato and bell pepper in a bowl.
Drizzle with the tahini dressing and toss to combine.

name: Salmon with Asparagus
method: Preheat the oven to 200C (400F).
Mince the garlic and arrange the salmon and asparagus on a lined baking tray.
Drizzle with olive oil and scatter over the garlic.
Roast for 12-15 minutes until the salmon is cooked through.
Finish with a squeeze of lemon before serving.

name: Turkey Meatballs with Rice
method: Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
In a bowl, combine the ground turkey, breadcrumbs, egg, onion and garlic. Mix well and shape into meatballs.
Heat a pan over medium heat and cook the meatballs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned and cooked through.
Meanwhile, cook the rice according to packet instructions.
Serve the meatballs over the rice.

name: Beef Stir-Fry
method: Cook the rice according to packet instructions.
Slice the beef thinly and mince the garlic.
Heat the sesame oil in a wok or large pan over high heat.
Stir-fry the beef for 2-3 minutes until browned, then set aside.
Add the broccoli, bell pepper and snap peas to the pan and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Return the beef to the pan with the garlic and soy sauce, and toss everything together for 1-2 minutes.
Serve over the cooked rice.

name: Protein Energy Balls
method: Combine the peanut butter, honey and protein powder in a bowl and mix until smooth.
Fold in the oats until evenly combined.
Roll the mixture into small balls.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up before eating.
